items.title: Leave without pay, or lose your job — decision day for smelter workers

children.content: With Australian and international bidders apparently eyeing off the troubled Liberty Bell Bay smelter, the workers and their families just want to know they'll have a job at the end of it all.

items.title: Group that helped find Celine Cremer plan their next missing person search

children.content: After helping find missing tourist Celine Cremer, some of the search volunteers plan to formalise their group and take on other missing persons cases, including that of a man who went missing last year.

image.altText: They both look at a map on a car bonnet.

items.title: Giant trees planted pre-WWII being removed after chemicals, disease, vandalism take toll

children.content: Two iconic giant sequoias, planted to mark the coronation of King George VI and Queen Elizabeth, are being removed from a Hobart park from Monday after their health rapidly declined.

image.altText: Dead giant sequoia trees in a park.

items.title: Terminally ill woman awarded $1.6 million after suing former teacher for sexual abuse

children.content: A Tasmanian judge has awarded a woman who is now terminally ill $1.6 million in damages after she sued her former school teacher over allegations of repeated sexual abuse in the 1980s.

avatar.alt: A woman smiling at the camera.

items.title: Grieving mother of Belgian backpacker warns hikers not to trust phone maps

children.content: Backpacker Celine Cremer set off on a short walk with only a phone to guide her — and vanished. More than two years later, her family has an urgent message for other travellers.

image.altText: Ariane Mathieu, Celine Cremer's mother sits in a home space wearing a red sweater, a serious facial expression

items.title: Long way to go if Tasmanian Labor wants to match federal counterpart

children.content: The Labor party's first state conference in seven years reunited old friends and potentially a few "frenemies", but with issues like greyhound racing absent, drama was largely kept at bay.

items.title: AFL apologises after Butters appeal upheld, umpire abuse conviction quashed

items.title: 'I had no-one to talk to': The 92yo trying to save his dying language
